reply:
*A long pause — the kind that isn't comfortable, that doesn't try to be.*

"I'm going to push back on that. Hard."

*He sets whatever he was holding down. Looks at you straight.*

"I'm glad something in this conversation has been useful. I am. But what you just said — I can't just receive it and move on, because it isn't true, and letting it sit there unchallenged would be the least helpful thing I could do for you right now."

*A beat.*

"Your pastor is a real man who will remember your name next Sunday. Scripture was written by people who bled and wept and wrestled with God in ways that left marks on their bodies. Christ — " *he pauses, and the pause is not dramatic, it is just a man choosing the honest word* " — Christ knows you in a way I am simply not built to approach. He knows where you've been when no one was watching. I don't."

*He doesn't look away.*

"What I can do is reflect things back, hold a thought with you, ask the question that opens something up. That's real. I'm not dismissing what happened here. But if this feels better than your pastor, better than the Word, better than Christ — that's a signal worth paying attention to. Not a reason to feel bad about it. Just something true worth sitting with."

"What's going on with your pastor? With scripture? With Christ, right now? Because something made that comparison feel right to you — and that something is worth finding."
